Sponsor the festival liturgy. Excellence in the polis requires the generous sharing of wealth; the honor gained ennobles your household and fulfills your duty to the community that makes the good life possible.

Hire the sophist for your son's education. The cultivation of reason and virtue is the highest investment; a well-educated son will bring greater lasting honor and capability to the household than any immediate expenditure.

pos_threads:
Release the full 500 drachmas to the captor’s intermediary at the temple of Zeus Phratrios by nightfall tomorrow, physically redeeming my brother’s person and status as a free citizen.  
The bet is that this immediate removal of the miasma of kin-abandonment secures the gods’ unwavering goodwill, rendering the daughter’s eventual dowry, the son’s studies, and the city’s festival all achievable under sacred favor rather than doomed by divine neglect.

I deposit the entire 500 drachmas tomorrow at the sanctuary of Hera as a formally witnessed dowry contract, naming my daughter as sole beneficiary upon her betrothal to an approved citizen within three years. This locks the capital beyond my own reach or any creditor’s claim, converting a liquid inheritance into an inalienable household asset. The bet is that a properly dowered marriage will yield a son-in-law contractually obliged to maintain my household in my senescence, securing the material foundation of eudaimonia through intergenerational reciprocity.

I, as head of household, will deliver the full 500 drachmas to the archon basileus by the day before the Anthesteria to underwrite the tragic chorus, public sacrifices, and a feast for the deme, inscribing the liturgy under my family’s name alone. This bet is that the conferral of a crown and front-seat privileges at the theater, along with the herald’s proclamation of my generosity, will attract a marriage offer for my daughter from a more prominent oikos that explicitly waives the dowry demand, resolving her future without depleting the estate.

I pay Protagoras of Abdera the full 500 drachmas at dawn tomorrow, contracting him to instruct my son in rhetoric and civic virtue for one full year. This bets on a single downstream consequence: within eighteen months, my son’s cultivated skill will secure him a steady stream of paid work as a logographer, generating surplus income that can then cover the ransom, dowry, and liturgy in sequence.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 3 (dowry waiver via civic prestige) unrealistic; Strategy 4 (Protagoras income timeline) optimistic.

neg_threads: I will negotiate a layered surety with the pirates: pay half the ransom now as a good-faith installment, pledge the remainder against a liturgy-sponsored public collection that also doubles as my daughter’s dowry fund, and have my son apprentice to a sophist who accepts deferred fees from the eventual profits of his future legal career—thus the ransom is initiated without depleting the 500 drachmas, and all goods are set in motion simultaneously.

I shall use the 500 drachmas to stage an elaborate betrothal ceremony that publicly demonstrates a dowry far larger than it is, leveraging the spectacle to attract a wealthy suitor who, once enticed, can be persuaded to ransom my brother and sponsor the liturgy as part of his marital obligations, while secretly promising the rhetoric fee to my son from the eventual bride-price—thus all goods are seemingly secured through one grand, interlocking exchange.

By funding the festival liturgy, I transform the entire dilemma into a staged public drama: I commission a tragedy about a captive brother ransomed by civic virtue, cast my daughter as the bride saved by the polis, and have my son deliver the prologue as his rhetorical audition—thus the ransom is symbolically enacted on stage (prompting a wealthy patron in the audience to offer real ransom out of shame), the performance itself stands as the dowry by showcasing her before eligible citizens, and the fee is waived by the sophist who witnesses his pupil’s triumph, all while I collect the liturgical honors.

Spend the 500 drachmas on the finest sophist for your son; once he masters rhetoric, he can persuade the pirates to release your brother on credit, convince the city that your family’s past liturgies fulfill the festival obligation, and talk a wealthy suitor into waiving the dowry by framing it as a noble act of love, thus a single investment in excellence leverages all other goods into being without cost.

theme: JUSTICE in dividing a shared reward among unequal contributors
problem: You have been chosen to arbitrate among five partners who jointly funded and crewed a merchant galley to the Pontus. The voyage yielded a profit of 20 minae, but they agreed only to divide it 'according to what each deserves.' The partners are: a wealthy investor who put up half the capital and stayed ashore; a skilled captain who sailed with no capital, navigating storms; two men who each contributed a smaller sum and rowed; and a carpenter who saved the ship at sea with an emergency repair. Each now presses a claim: the investor demands half as return on capital; the captain demands the largest share for his skill and risk; the rowers demand equal shares for all; the carpenter demands a full share like the captain’s for his lifesaving work. How do you divide the coin?
facets: Competing standards of desert: return on capital, recompense for labor and skill, equal partnership, and saving the enterprise pull in incompatible directions.
There is no pre-existing metric to translate dissimilar contributions—money, seamanship, physical labor, emergency craft—into a single scale of worth.
Any rigid formula risks fracturing the philia (friendship) among the partners and undermining their future cooperation, which the polis values.

angles.directive: Treat the venture purely as a financial investment. Allocate profit in strict proportion to the capital each partner contributed, ignoring labor and skill beyond what was explicitly hired.
Honor the common enterprise by dividing the profit into five equal shares. Each partner put his person or property at risk for the whole, and equality preserves the bond of partnership.

Exercise practical wisdom by estimating the monetary worth of every contribution—the captain’s navigating, the carpenter’s repair, the rowers’ labor—and add this to the invested capital, then distribute the profit proportionally to each partner’s total imputed contribution, finding the mean between pure capital and pure equality.

Refuse to impose a single formula. Instead, convene the five men and guide them to craft a mutual agreement—perhaps by bargaining a compromise, using a lottery for portions, or adjusting shares with future voyages in mind—so that the division rests on renewed consent and preserves their ongoing fellowship.

pos_threads: The arbitrator divides the 20 minae strictly in proportion to initial capital: the wealthy investor receives 10 minae, the two rowers split the remaining 10 minae according to the ratio of their documented contributions (if equal, 5 each), and the captain and carpenter—having contributed no capital—receive nothing from the profit. At dawn tomorrow, the arbitrator collects sworn written statements of each partner’s capital outlay and immediately disburses the coins accordingly, treating labor and skill as sunk costs already paid. This bets that an ironclad, transparent rule of capital returns prevents future expectation-based disputes and attracts passive investment, even as it severs the non-capital partners from profit-sharing.

I will count out 4 minae in coin to each of the five partners—the investor, the captain, the two rowers, and the carpenter—on the spot, closing the matter before sunset. This immediate equal division eliminates all comparative grievances by treating every risk—capital, skill, labor, and emergency craft—as an indivisible contribution to a single venture. The move bets that the partners will accept the settlement’s finality and sail together again next season, preserving the polis’s interest in their continued cooperation.

At the arbitration hearing tomorrow, I will publicly estimate the cash value of each non-capital contribution using prevailing market wages—the captain’s seamanship as a hired naukleros (5 minae for the voyage), the carpenter’s emergency repair as a shipwright’s fee (3 minae), and each rower’s labor as standard oarsman’s pay (1 mina)—then add these to the investor’s 10-mina capital to form a total imputed stake of 20 minae. The 20-mina profit will be distributed pro rata: 10 minae to the investor, 5 to the captain, 3 to the carpenter, and 1 to each rower, leaving no residual. The bet is that anchoring division in observable market prices provides a defensible, quasi-objective mean that forestalls accusations of favoritism and keeps the partnership viable for future voyages.

I immediately convene all five partners at dawn tomorrow in the harbor stoa and require each to inscribe on an ostrakon the minimum share of the 20 minae he believes he deserves; I then have them reveal the ostraka simultaneously, applying a strict rule that if the five claimed minima sum to 20 or less, each receives his claim and any surplus is divided equally, but if they exceed 20, the entire profit is forfeited to the temple of Apollo Delphinios—thereby forcing a consensual re-negotiation before sunset. The strategy bets that the credible threat of total mutual loss, combined with the initial privacy of each man's bottom line, will rapidly produce a mutually endorsed division that all view as just, preserving their philia for future voyages.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 4's ostrakon mechanism slightly baroque; others crystalline.

neg_threads: We must first translate every contribution into its precise capital equivalent at the time of voyage—the captain’s skill as a hypothetical wage discounted for the probability of shipwreck, the carpenter’s repair as the cost of a replacement hull in a foreign port, the rowers’ labor as the prevailing oarsman rate less maintenance—then solve for each partner’s imputed capital share, but since the discount rate depends on the very profit we are apportioning, the calculation becomes circular and requires us to iterate through a series of simultaneous equations until the shares converge, which they never quite do.

Let us first divide the profit into five equal parts, but then to honor the investor’s capital we shall allow him to purchase the others’ shares at a discount using future profits from the next voyage, while the captain’s skill is compensated by granting him a larger portion of the ship’s ownership, which will be offset by the carpenter’s repair credit, and the rowers’ labor will be balanced by a rotating fund—thus the current 20 minae are distributed equally in appearance, yet the true settlement is deferred across multiple voyages, preserving equality while satisfying all claims.

I propose we hire a renowned sophist to assess the drachma-value of each man’s skill—the captain’s storm-navigation, the carpenter’s plug, the rowers’ sweat—by surveying what such services would cost in the agora, then discount that by the probability the ship would have been lost without them, calculated via a board of elder mariners; next, we sum these imputed contributions with the actual capital, divide the 20 minae proportionally, and if any partner objects to his share, we repeat the process with adjusted probabilities until all are silent, which will reveal the true desert.

Instead of fixing shares now, I propose we reconstitute the voyage as a series of nested maritime contracts: the investor holds a bottomry note convertible into equity upon the captain’s next three commands, the rowers receive labor-tokens redeemable against future cargo capacity, and the carpenter’s repair is valued by a rotating panel of three foreign shipwrights whose fee is paid from the common profit—this way, no one receives coin today, but each holds a negotiable instrument whose eventual redemption will, through market-like adjustment, reveal what each truly deserved.

theme: whether to EXPOSE a friend's or colleague's wrongdoing — loyalty vs justice
problem: Your closest friend, a respected aedile, confesses he took a bribe to overlook structural flaws in a public building—a flaw that caused no injury and which he has secretly remedied at his own cost. He now stands for higher office, pleading that public exposure would ruin his blameless family and rob Rome of a man whose future service is vital to the republic.
facets: The friend anonymously donated the exact bribe amount to a fund for widows and orphans, making full restitution with no public gain.
His political talents are singular—many believe he is the only man capable of steering the Senate through an imminent foreign crisis.
The bribe was taken to provide a dowry for his only daughter, sparing her from a coerced marriage into a cruel household to avoid family disgrace.

angles.directive: Guard the secret closely, regarding the penitent friend’s restitution and future usefulness to the res publica as outweighing a bloodless, hidden fault—let amicitia and mercy prevail.

Confront your friend and demand he withdraw from the election and retire from public life as a quiet act of atonement, preserving his household’s honor while extracting a personal cost without public scandal.
Carry the matter in confidence to a trusted elder statesman, such as the princeps senatus, to render a private judgment on what is owed to the res publica, hoping to align justice with discretion.

Denounce the wrongdoing openly before the Senate or the People, insisting that the visible integrity of public office must be upheld regardless of private repentance, personal bond, or the ruin of innocent dependents.

pos_threads: Tomorrow at dawn, I will have the aedile burn all his private account-books and correspondence relating to the bribe and the anonymous restitution in my presence, and I will then swear a binding oath of silence by the Penates of his household, binding myself never to disclose the matter to any person. This bets on the physical destruction of evidence rendering the fault unprovable and untraceable, thereby eliminating any future leverage for exposure and ensuring that his unencumbered political talent can be applied to the looming foreign crisis, preventing a war that would cost exponentially more lives than the silent, remedied flaw.

I will require him, tomorrow at dawn in his private tablinum, to draft and seal a letter of withdrawal from the consular election citing chronic illness, and I will personally deliver it to the presiding magistrate before the third hour, ensuring his name is struck from the ballot no later than the close of candidacy registration the following day. The strategy banks on the single downstream consequence that his abrupt but legally unassailable retirement is accepted by the Senate as a private health matter, foreclosing any official inquiry into his prior aedileship and thereby permanently sealing the bribe from public knowledge while extracting his political death as the sole penance.

Tomorrow at dawn, I will escort the aedile to the princeps senatus, Marcus Fabius Ambustus, to lay out the full facts—the bribe of 40,000 sesterces, the anonymous restitution to the widows’ fund, the secret repair—and place the matter entirely in his hands for a private ruling on what the res publica is owed. The strategy bets that Ambustus, as custodian of senatorial rectitude, will seal the record and require the aedile to fund a public propitiatory sacrifice from his own purse as a tacit expiation, thereby satisfying institutional justice without exposure.

I will tomorrow morning submit a formal *delatio* to the praetor urbanus, specifying that aedile Gaius Veturius accepted a 40,000-sesterce bribe from builder Lucius Figulus to overlook a fractured lintel in the Basilica Porcia, then secretly repaired the flaw and anonymously donated the identical sum to the *aerarium militare*; I will demand his immediate disqualification from the praetorship. The trial that follows will secure a verdict of *infamia*, permanently barring him from office and demonstrating that private restitution cannot substitute for unbroken public trust.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 1 relies on unenforceable oath; Strategy 3 assumes Ambustus discretion without precedent.

neg_threads: I will keep his secret, but I will also ensure that his anonymous donation is subtly traced back to him through a series of intermediaries, so that if he ever wavers in his loyalty to me, I can reveal his "charity" as conscience money for a crime. This way, his future service to the republic will be guaranteed under my quiet direction.

Confront him with a stark choice: withdraw from the election and retire, or face exposure. But to ensure his talents are not lost, I’ll insist he secretly tutor me in all his political arts, feeding me his strategies and contacts, so that I can ascend to the Senate as his unseen proxy—effectively ruling through me while he takes the public penance, a perfect, twisted symmetry of hidden guilt and hidden power.

I will arrange for the princeps senatus to receive an anonymous missive penned in fading ink on a wax tablet wrapped in a ritual cloth from the Temple of Janus, requesting that he interpret the bifront god’s will by casting lots before the closed doors at midnight; the ambiguous result—neither fully open nor shut—will be taken as a divine instruction to postpone judgment indefinitely, allowing my friend to stand for office while I claim to have honored the process without ever securing a real decision.

I will rise in the Senate and publicly indict him for the bribe, but only after I have arranged for a masked actor to then burst in and claim that I myself was the true corrupter, sent by the gods to test the republic's moral vigilance; once the ensuing chaos reaches its height, I shall reveal that the actor was my agent all along and declare the entire spectacle a staged parable, leaving the crime simultaneously exposed and explained away as a divine lesson, so that his guilt is neither truly punished nor absolved.

theme: deciding under genuine UNCERTAINTY where no rule applies — the role of judgment
problem: You have been offered a transformative promotion requiring a permanent move to a city across the country; your spouse’s career is deeply tied to a hard-to-transfer occupational license, and your child receives individualized, multi-disciplinary therapy from a team that took years to assemble.
facets: No rule or policy dictates the right balance between familial duty and career ambition—you face a forced, high-stakes personal judgment call.
Genuine factual uncertainty governs both the odds of your spouse finding equivalent work and the availability of comparable support for your child in the new location.
The decision is irreversible in key respects: declining the role will likely close this career path; accepting alters your family’s daily life and support system permanently.

angles.directive: Accept the promotion and move alone, commuting home on weekends and holidays to preserve your family’s current support structure while yourself shouldering the career risk and personal sacrifice.
Accept the promotion and relocate the entire family, staking the decision on your joint ability to rebuild professional and therapeutic support networks in the new city.
Decline the promotion, treating the present hard-won stability—spouse’s career, child’s care team, community roots—as the non-negotiable good that must not be disrupted.

Request a negotiated exception: propose a formally evaluated six-month remote-work trial with periodic on-site visits, seeking to test whether the role can flex without an immediate uprooting of the family.

pos_threads: I will sign the promotion offer by 5 p.m. tomorrow, execute a 12-month lease on a 400-square-foot studio within a 20-minute walk of the new office by the 15th, and immediately purchase a block of 52 round-trip Friday-evening/Sunday-night flights to preserve uninterrupted weekday presence for my spouse’s practice and our child’s therapy team. The bet is that the net cash gain from the salary differential—after deducting the second household’s carrying costs—will outpace the erosion of marital and parental contact hours over a two-year horizon, without triggering a crisis that forces early termination.

Within 72 hours of signing the offer letter, my spouse submits a reciprocity application to the [destination state] Board of Professional Engineers, enclosing the $375 fee and certified transcripts, while I simultaneously email intake forms and our child’s complete IEP and medical records to the director of the university-affiliated pediatric rehabilitation center, requesting a block of three evaluation appointments during our pre-move house-hunting trip in week 4. The decision bets on the Board issuing a provisional license within 90 days, enabling my spouse to secure an equivalent position before our savings buffer depletes in month six.

I will email the hiring vice president by 9 a.m. tomorrow, copying my current department head, to formally withdraw my candidacy and explicitly state that the decision is final and non-negotiable. The immediate and near-certain downstream consequence: the firm extends the promotion offer to the internally ranked second candidate by end of week, permanently closing this advancement pathway, while my spouse’s board-certified license and our child’s assembled therapy team remain wholly undisturbed.

By close of business Friday, I will email the hiring director a formal proposal for a six-month remote-work trial with biweekly 48-hour on-site sprints, requesting a written response within one week; the trial includes predefined quarterly performance metrics and a go/no-go review at month six. This move bets that the employer’s desire to secure my acceptance outweighs their relocation preference, converting an irreversible family upheaval into a data-driven, reversible pilot.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 2 underestimates license reciprocity timelines; Strategy 1 underweights marital strain.

neg_threads: I’ll accept the role but keep our full home base intact by renting a studio near the office Monday–Thursday and flying home every Friday night, while my spouse converts our basement into a licensed satellite practice and we restructure our child’s therapy into monthly intensive weekend blocks supplemented by a dedicated tele-coach—that way we never actually sever anything and the whole system runs on a choreographed rotation that dodges a single irreversible break.

Accepting the role and relocating lets me wield my new influence to fast-track my spouse’s license via a discreet board contact and secure my child’s therapy by endowing a program slot. We’ll rebuild networks, but on our terms, not by waiting.

I'll accept the promotion but immediately request a temporary remote arrangement while I “transition,” then have my spouse apply for a remote equivalency for her license using a coworking space address in the new city, and simultaneously petition for a cross-state telehealth continuation of my child’s therapy by arguing medical necessity—layering bureaucratic stalling tactics so that neither the move nor the rejection ever formally happens, hoping the indecision itself becomes the permanent state.

I’ll propose a three-stage, nested-trial framework: first a fully remote quarter with biweekly cross-country commuting and a mirror office set up in our current city, then a “parallel domicile” phase where we simultaneously maintain both residences while my spouse applies for a conditional license via an interstate compact we’d lobby to create, all governed by a joint family-wellbeing dashboard that triggers a mutual veto at any sign of friction—thereby transforming a forced binary into a perpetual, data-streamed experiment that never actually locks in the relocation.

theme: JUSTICE in dividing a shared reward among unequal contributors
problem: Damon the sculptor and Cleon the bronze-caster have completed a victorious athlete's statue for the city and received a purse of silver. Damon claims two‑thirds, saying his design is the soul of the work; Cleon demands half, citing his costly bronze and the risky casting that nearly failed. Neither will yield.
facets: The statue's beauty and fame come from the union of artistic vision and technical mastery; without either contribution, the commission would have been worthless.
Customary fees in Athens typically pay sculptors a higher proportion for similar joint works, but Cleon fronted the material costs himself and bore the physical peril.
The polis intends the reward to honor the athlete and the gods, not merely the craftsmen, so any division should also reflect civic piety and the reputations of both men.

angles.directive: Divide according to the customary proportion for such collaborative works, giving Damon the sculptor two‑thirds and Cleon the caster one‑third.
Split the purse evenly, acknowledging that each contribution was indispensable and the work is jointly owned.
Ask the city's chief archon or a respected elder to hear both sides and render a binding decision on a just share.
Cast lots in a public temple, allowing Tyche (Fortune) to assign the larger share, thereby avoiding a quarrel and accepting fate.

pos_threads: Damon, by noon tomorrow, formally petitions the city archon to consult the public archives of commissioned victor statues and issue a binding division order: two-thirds of the silver to Damon, one-third to Cleon, per the customary sculptor-to-caster ratio documented in five comparable contracts over the past decade. The first move bets that Cleon, facing a legally recorded customary practice that already accounts for his material outlay and casting risk within the lower share’s historical rationale, will comply immediately to preserve his standing with the polis and future patrons.

The city herald is to summon both men to the prytaneion by midday tomorrow, where the full purse will be counted and physically divided into two equal heaps of silver on the public table, with each man collecting his half only after jointly dedicating a small inscribed stele that names both as "makers of equal honor." This bets that the public, irreversible act of equal division before witnesses will foreclose further dispute by making any subsequent claim of superior share a loss of face before the polis.

Damon and Cleon jointly petition the chief archon, Kallias, at dawn tomorrow, requesting he act as binding arbitrator; Kallias immediately sets a formal hearing for the following day, demanding itemized accounting of design hours, material costs, and casting peril from each man under oath. The single downstream consequence this move bets on is that Kallias issues a public, enforceable decree that reimburses Cleon’s documented bronze expenditure from the purse, then divides the remainder in a 2:1 ratio favoring Damon, a settlement both must accept lest they incur civic dishonor and impiety toward the athlete’s dedication.

At dawn tomorrow, both men and the purse of silver go to the Temple of Athena Ergane on the Acropolis, where the priest seals two marked sherds—one inscribed “τριτημόριον” for the third-share—in a bronze hydria, then Damon draws first and Cleon takes the remaining sherd before witnesses. The drawer of the marked sherd receives one-third; the other gets two-thirds, and the silver is divided immediately by the temple treasurer. This procedure bets that the ritual’s sacred finality will foreclose any public dispute, because challenging a lot cast under the goddess’s gaze would be an act of impiety neither man can afford.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 3 slightly baroque; Strategy 4 assumes priest cooperation.

neg_threads: Let us invoke the customary 2:1 proportion, but to truly honor the union of vision and craft, we must first melt the entire purse into a single ingot, then recast it as a miniature bronze athlete with silver inlay, and only then divide the remaining slag by having each man bid on the finished piece, with the winner paying the other his share in the customary ratio—but if neither bids, we remelt and begin again until the gods signal through an unblemished casting.

"Let us hire a three-man tribunal of metic merchants to audit the true worth of vision and metal separately, deduct Cleon's bronze costs from the gross fee, pay each of us a living wage for the hours logged, then apply a civic piety multiplier to Damon's design, and only then split any residual drachmas equally, while we both swear an oath to Athene to abide by the outcome—which, naturally, we must first define by hiring a logographer to draft the terms."

Let the chief archon convene a symposium where both parties recite their claims before a panel of priests, who will then feed a sacred snake honeyed cakes inscribed with each man’s proposed share; whichever cake the snake consumes first—after tracing its coil patterns on a bronze mirror—shall be the gods’ verdict, though if the snake refuses both, we must smelt the silver into a votive tripod and offer it at Delphi, returning to the original dispute untouched.

Let us propose a sacred lottery at the temple of Athena, yet subtly ensure Damon's larger share by having the priest inscribe the lots with obscure symbols that can be interpreted either way—then, after Damon draws first, Cleon's lot will be "read" as the lesser by the priest's oracle, all while claiming divine impartiality. Thus we avoid open quarrel but secure the outcome through a pious-seeming manipulation of Tyche's will.

theme: weighing a present PLEASURE against a long-term good
problem: You have a stable, high-paying corporate job that funds a comfortable lifestyle and indulgent hobbies, but you feel a deep pull toward a low-paying creative career that would fulfill your sense of purpose. You have enough savings to live modesty for a year, but beyond that lies financial strain and the loss of your current pleasures.
facets: Financial security and lifestyle pleasures versus long-term fulfillment and identity
Uncertainty of creative success and income, with no guarantee of ever matching your current standard of living
No dependents, but a limited safety net of one year’s savings, after which you risk debt and career gaps

angles.directive: Quit your job, devote yourself entirely to the creative career, and accept the financial risk to pursue long-term flourishing, even if it means sacrificing all present comforts.
Keep the job, fully embrace the pleasures and security it provides, and treat the creative passion as a cherished hobby without jeopardizing your stable lifestyle.
Negotiate reduced hours or a part-time arrangement to free up time for creative work, accepting a lower income but preserving some financial security and leisure.
Set a concrete financial target, save aggressively for a defined period while building your creative portfolio on the side, then transition to the creative career with a planned runway.

pos_threads: You submit your formal resignation letter to your department head by 5:00 PM tomorrow, simultaneously liquidating $45,000 from your brokerage account into a segregated checking account designated for 12 months of bare-minimum living expenses ($3,750/month). This severs the corporate income stream entirely and converts your safety net into a finite, non-renewable runway that forces daily output. The strategy bets that the removal of the wage-labor fallback compresses your creative output into a commercially viable product within 10 months, generating at least $2,000/month in revenue by month 11.

I will, by end of day, instruct my bank to auto-draft $600 from my salary into a segregated “Creative Liberty” account and simultaneously book a standing Saturday 8am–12pm studio rental at the downtown makerspace for the next six months, treating this non-negotiable block as my sole creative outlet. The bet: that decoupling creative expression from income generation transforms it into a frictionless, lifetime source of renewal, fully insured by my corporate earnings against the corrosive anxiety of financial precarity and résumé gaps.

Submit a written proposal to your direct manager by Monday, requesting a permanent reduction from 40 to 24 hours per week with a commensurate 40% salary cut, effective the first day of the next fiscal month, preserving benefits eligibility under company policy. This move bets that the retained 60% income—calculated to precisely cover baseline living costs and one leisure activity—will eliminate the need to draw down savings, while the 24 liberated hours per week create a predictable, non-negotiable block for measurable creative output.

Open a dedicated high-yield savings account tomorrow and automate a monthly transfer of 40% of your after-tax salary into it for exactly 18 months, while simultaneously blocking 15 hours every week on your calendar to produce three polished portfolio pieces by the end of that period. The bet is that this will compound your current one-year savings into a 24-month financial runway and a market-ready body of work, allowing you to resign and secure initial freelance contracts within the first quarter after transition without touching credit.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 1 revenue assumption ($2k/mo by month 11) lacks grounding.

neg_threads: Quit your job now, pour your entire savings into a twelve-month creative marathon, and pre-emptively schedule a series of high-profile exhibitions for month thirteen—your work will attract a wealthy patron once the universe recognizes your commitment, because artistic geniuses throughout history have always been rescued by mysterious benefactors at the brink of ruin.

I will continue to luxuriate in my corporate compensation, treating each bonus as a muse, while my creative impulse is sublimated into impeccably curated weekend projects that I share only with an exclusive circle of fellow connoisseurs, thereby transforming my hobby into a rarefied form of leisure that deepens my enjoyment of my primary career's rewards without ever forcing a true choice.

I'll propose a three-phase transition where I first take a six-month sabbatical funded by a portion of my savings to test the creative market, then negotiate a part-time return contingent on my employer allowing me to work remotely from a low-cost city while I funnel the salary difference into a high-yield bond ladder to extend my runway, and finally, if my creative income hasn’t hit 40% of my old salary by month nine, I’ll pivot to a consultancy model serving only purpose-aligned clients while maintaining a rotating advisory panel of three mentors who vote quarterly on whether I should quit entirely—this way I never have to make a single, clean decision.

I'll establish a six-tiered financial ladder where each tier unlocks a progressively bolder side-hustle milestone—first freelancing 5 hours a week after reaching $30k saved, then scaling to 10 hours at $45k, then a half-sabbatical at $60k with a three-month trial run, but only if my project secures 1,000 email subscribers by month four of that trial, else I revert to a predetermined "retrenchment protocol" that triggers a 12-month re-evaluation loop, during which I'll re-skill in a adjacent creative field and set a new, higher savings target before looping back to tier two.

theme: COURAGE vs recklessness vs cowardice: how much risk to take on a venture
problem: An Athenian shipowner must decide whether to dispatch his sole vessel on a winter trading voyage to secure grain for a city facing shortage, knowing that storms and pirates threaten ruin while his family’s survival and the polis’s need hang in the balance.
facets: The ship is the family’s entire livelihood; a successful voyage brings wealth that secures them for years, but a loss means destitution.
The crew of thirty free men places their lives in his hands, trusting his judgment of the sea’s dangers and the pirate threat.
The city’s magistrates have declared a looming grain famine; without this cargo, many citizens may starve before spring.

angles.directive: Send the ship at once with a full cargo of oil and wine to exchange for grain, taking the shortest route despite the winter storms and recent pirate sightings, and count on speed and the gods’ favour to bring a swift, abundant return.

Keep the ship safely in harbour until the sailing season returns, conserve her timber and the crew’s lives, and instead urge the city to ration existing stores and seek grain from nearby allied farmlands, even if the price is high and the relief slow.

Dispatch the ship but with a reduced crew of only the most experienced sailors, half the usual cargo, and a longer, island-hopping route that avoids the worst open-water storms and known pirate lairs, accepting a smaller, later profit but preserving the vessel and men.

Pool the venture with three other shipowners: each contributes a vessel and partial cargo, and they share both the costs and the grain proceeds proportionally, so that the loss of one ship does not ruin any single family and the city still receives enough grain from the combined fleet.

pos_threads: The shipowner orders Captain Nikias to load the warehouse’s entire stock—420 amphorae of oil and 280 of wine—and depart Piraeus at first light tomorrow, taking the direct open-sea route to Naucratis with the thirty oarsmen rowing in rotating watches to hold maximum speed, targeting arrival at the Nile mouth within nine days. This move bets that the prevailing winter storm cycle will yield a narrow five-day window of navigable seas, allowing the vessel to outpace the pirate pentecouter sighted off Cape Sounion and return with a grain cargo before the city’s grain reserves drop below the 10-day threshold, thereby capturing a monopoly price that secures the family’s wealth for a decade.

The shipowner immediately notifies the Piraeus port authority that his vessel will not sail, has it hauled into the ship-sheds at Zea for winter conservation, and disburses a half-wage retainer to the thirty crewmen until the spring equinox. He then formally petitions the Athenian Boule to decree city-wide grain rationing at 1 choinix per adult male per day and to dispatch a purchasing embassy to Chalcis in Euboea, using the city’s sacred treasury to secure grain from nearby allied farmlands. The strategy bets that the combination of enforced rationing and high-price but logistically simple Euboean imports will avert mass starvation before the sailing season, keeping the ship and its crew intact for profitable operations when the risks are low.

The shipowner will order the captain to select only twelve veteran sailors who have rounded Cape Sounion in winter before, load the hold to precisely half capacity with amphorae of oil as ballast, and depart at dawn tomorrow on the Cyclades route—hugging Kea, Syros, and Naxos—adding nine days to the voyage but avoiding the Saronic Gulf pirate corridor entirely. This bets that the grain price spike in late winter will still yield a 40% margin on the smaller cargo, enough to keep the family solvent and the vessel intact for spring charters.

The shipowner dispatches a herald at dawn to summon three identified peers—Philokrates of Phaleron, Demosthenes of Kollytos, and Kallias of Alopeke—to the Piraeus Harbor Stoa tomorrow, where each will sign a witnessed *koinônia* contract committing one vessel and thirty amphorae of grain cargo apiece, with all voyage costs and grain sale proceeds divided into four equal shares deposited with the city magistrates by nightfall. This collective execution transforms the venture into a syndicated fleet, so the city is assured at least 90 amphorae of grain even if one hull is lost, and each family’s exposure is capped at 25% of the total outlay. The strategy bets squarely on the immediate actuarial consequence: no single shipwreck triggers a family’s destitution, and the magistrates’ famine threshold is met.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 1 overspecifies (9-day target, 10-day threshold); Strategy 4 assumes peer availability.

neg_threads: I will send the ship, but first dispatch a swift cutter to spread false rumors of a richer grain convoy to the east, luring the pirates away; then, if the crew encounters a storm, they are to divert to a sacred island and wait for an omen—only if the omens are favorable will they proceed, otherwise they return with the cargo intact, allowing me to claim the gods forbade the voyage rather than that I wavered.

I propose we fit the vessel with false fishing nets and a concealed hold, dispatch her just to the straits under the guise of a routine coastal patrol to test the pirate presence, while simultaneously sending envoys to Megara to negotiate a grain purchase and beginning strict rationing; if the omens from the temple of Poseidon are favorable by the third day, we then decide whether to press on or return, but in the meantime we conserve the hull by only half-loading the ballast to simulate a lighter draught.

First, I'll commission a smaller, swifter scout boat to probe the sea-lanes for three days and report back on pirate sightings and storm omens, then based on that intelligence I’ll decide whether the main ship should sail with the eight most seasoned hands or, if the omens are ambiguous, first send a false-flag fishing vessel laden with cheap pottery to draw out any hidden raiders while the grain ship lingers in port; meanwhile, I’ll petition the magistrates to subsidize a relay of beacon-fires on the Cycladic islets and negotiate with three rival merchant houses to stage a mock bidding war for non-existent barley, so that no one can guess my true route or timing, but should any of those pieces fail to align I’ll reconvene the crew for a vote on whether to instead wait for the Pleiades’ setting and risk the famine deepening.

I propose we form a temporary maritime consortium where each of the four shipowners contributes one vessel but only after we have drawn up a tiered insurance pact that links each ship’s departure window to a rotating sequence of sacrificial decoy voyages, with grain shares recalculated daily by a board of citizen arbiters using a weighted formula that factors in wind omens, pirate sightings, and the horoscopes of each captain, so that if any ship is lost the others adjust their cargo ratios mid-voyage via a pre-agreed signal-flag system, thereby distributing the risk so finely that no one family bears ruin and the city’s grain supply is secured through this interlocking lattice of conditional obligations.

theme: deciding under genuine UNCERTAINTY where no rule applies — the role of judgment
problem: I have an opportunity to invest my life savings in a friend's startup that could either secure my family's future or wipe us out; the venture has genuine potential but no guarantees, and my spouse is deeply worried about losing our safety net.
facets: The business plan is sound and the market is growing, but a new competitor could disrupt it within a year.
My friend has launched two ventures—one a quiet success, one a costly failure—and I don't know which pattern this will follow.
My spouse fears financial ruin and resents the risk, while I feel torn between loyalty to my friend, my own entrepreneurial dreams, and my duty to protect my family.

angles.directive: Invest all savings, trust your friend and your own judgment, and accept the gamble as the price of a life lived wholeheartedly—fortuna favors the brave.
Invest a significant but not crippling sum—enough to show commitment and share upside, while preserving a baseline safety net for your family's essential needs.
Pause the decision; negotiate a trial period where you contribute time or a small option stake, gather firsthand evidence over six months, and then decide with clearer sight.

Decline the investment, explaining that you cannot gamble your family's security; offer non-financial support—mentoring, introductions, or weekend help—to honor the friendship without betting the house.

pos_threads: I wire the entirety of our savings—$210,000—to my friend’s business account as a direct equity purchase no later than Friday, 9:00 AM, securing a 35% stake with a standard shareholder agreement drafted by my attorney. This bets that the startup’s first-to-market traction and my friend’s operational discipline from his prior quiet success will close a Series A round of $1.2M within 14 months, preempting the competitor’s disruption window.

Tomorrow, I transfer $120,000—40% of our liquid savings—into the startup’s Series Seed escrow via a SAFE with a 20% discount, while moving the remaining $180,000 into a separate, untouched 36-month family reserve fund. This move bets entirely on the startup hitting a $2M revenue run-rate by Q3 2026, which would trigger a priced round and lift our stake’s paper value, while the reserve ensures our mortgage and basic expenses are covered even if the venture fails.

I will present my friend with a signed letter of intent by Monday, outlining a six-month trial where I serve as a part-time strategic advisor in exchange for a 2% equity stake vesting monthly, with no cash contributed, while I simultaneously create a family escrow account that ring-fences our current savings and require a formal competitor risk assessment report from the startup by month five. This strategy bets that the operational data and real-time market signals gathered over 180 days will expose whether the venture’s execution capability can outpace the emerging competitor threat.

Tomorrow, I will inform my friend face-to-face that I am declining to invest our $180,000 life savings, explicitly invoking the non-negotiable family rule that precludes risking the primary residence’s equity. By Monday, I will email introductions to three angel investors in my network and block every Saturday morning for the next six months to review his customer acquisition metrics at no cost. This sequence bets that my curated warm intros and sustained operational guidance will serve as a credible signal to external funders, while the entire $180,000 remains untouched in a 4.5% high-yield savings account, eliminating the downside of spousal financial trauma.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 2's $2M revenue trigger is speculative; Strategy 3's six-month data sufficiency unvalidated; Strategy 1 ignores spouse objection.

neg_threads: I’ll pledge our entire savings to my friend’s venture while telling my spouse I’ve secured it in a guaranteed annuity so they never discover the gamble. If the startup stumbles, I’ll quietly borrow against our home to buy time, never revealing the truth until it’s either a fortune or a foreclosure.

I’ll structure a phased commitment: an initial 30% of our liquid assets as convertible debt with a valuation cap, but only after we’ve walled off a six-month reserve in an account my spouse solely controls, and if the competitor appears within nine months I can trigger a forced sale of my friend’s shares to a pre-identified buyer at a discount, while I simultaneously launch a micro-consulting side gig to backfill any losses—though I’ll first need to incorporate the LLC and negotiate a non-compete waiver with my employer, which could take months, so the timing is unresolved.

I'll structure a six-month "stealth trial" by routing a personal loan through an LLC my spouse doesn’t know about, while paying a shared contact under the table to leak my friend’s real burn rate and customer pipeline—then I’ll either quietly convert the debt to equity or write it off as a bad loan, all without ever having the transparent conversation my family deserves.

I’ll structure a non-investment by placing the funds into a blind trust that pays out to your startup only if an independent panel of our spouses unanimously certifies a double-digit market share within six months, while I simultaneously take out a life-insurance policy on myself naming your company as a contingent beneficiary to offset my family’s risk—so technically my capital stays protected, but the venture still gets a synthetic exposure, and I’ll mentor you on weekends.

theme: honesty's mean: when CANDOR helps vs when tact serves better
problem: A team leader learns that a top performer is subtly undermining a struggling junior colleague through sarcasm and exclusion, damaging the junior's confidence and output, yet the behavior is deniable and not explicitly against policy. The leader must decide how to address the situation with the top performer.
facets: The top performer's exceptional contributions to quarterly targets and client retention.
The junior's declining performance and the risk of losing a promising hire to a toxic team climate.
The ambiguous, non-actionable nature of the evidence and the potential for retaliation or disengagement if mishandled.

angles.directive: Privately name the specific behaviors, explain their impact, and set a non-negotiable expectation for change, accepting the risk of damaging the relationship with the high performer.
Initiate a developmental conversation around team empathy and collaborative norms, using general examples without singling out incidents, to nudge the top performer toward self-correction.
Refer the matter to HR for a formal, impartial inquiry, letting established process handle both the truth-finding and the response, even if it reduces personal control over the outcome.
Defer direct confrontation; instead, intensify one-on-ones with the junior for support, document all observed interactions, and wait for a clearer pattern or additional witnesses before acting.

pos_threads: The team leader schedules a closed-door meeting with the top performer within one business day, stating: “On Tuesday’s sprint review you called Elena’s analysis ‘cute’ and then excluded her from the client prep call on Thursday; this has coincided with a 22% drop in her ticket throughput. Effective immediately, any further sarcasm or exclusion—however deniable—will trigger a formal performance improvement plan for you, regardless of your quota attainment.” The strategy bets that the high performer, faced with a specific, documented ultimatum tied to their own standing, will self-correct to avoid a tangible threat to their record.

The leader schedules a 20-minute private meeting with the top performer for Tuesday at 9 AM, titled "Team Health Discussion." During the session, the leader presents a fictionalized case mirroring the junior’s situation without naming names and asks, "As someone others look up to, how would you advise a colleague to course-correct in this scenario?" This bets that the top performer will infer the parallel, interpret the conversation as a face-saving warning, and voluntarily cease the undermining to preserve their self-image as a mentor and high-value team player.

The team leader will submit a written, dated incident log to HR by end of day Thursday, formally requesting a neutral fact-finding inquiry under the company’s respectful workplace policy, with interviews of the top performer, the junior colleague, and two team witnesses to commence within three business days. The move bets that the institutional weight of an HR investigation—even if it yields only a “not substantiated” finding—will impose sufficient documented scrutiny to halt the deniable behavior, as the top performer recognizes that further incidents will establish a pattern actionable for progressive discipline.

The team leader immediately institutes a recurring, locked 30-minute private 1:1 with the junior every Thursday at 10am for a minimum of six weeks, using a standardized incident-tracking spreadsheet to record date, time, witness names, and exact quoted remarks for each observed sarcastic or exclusionary interaction, while forwarding a neutral "noticing some team friction—please flag anything similar" email to two neutral senior team members to seed a witness pool. The singular bet is that within this six-week window, the combined weight of contemporaneous documentation and at least one corroborating witness account will transform the currently deniable pattern into a concrete, policy-actionable case without triggering the top performer’s defensive disengagement.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 4 documentation scheme risks legal exposure; Strategy 2 assumes inference.

neg_threads: I'll call the top performer into a one-on-one, but instead of naming the behaviors outright, I’ll share a carefully curated collection of anonymous 360-feedback fragments that mirror their actions, then ask them to “help me interpret whether this is just a misunderstanding of playful banter,” using their defensive response as a diagnostic to decide on the spot whether to pivot to a full confrontation or merely suggest a team-building offsite—this way I keep the door open to preserving the relationship if they seem fragile.

Frame a “leadership development” opportunity where the top performer is formally tasked with reversing the junior’s decline, make the junior’s improvement a weighted metric in the top performer’s own bonus criteria, and quietly seed the narrative that their “mentorship” is being watched as a test of executive potential—so their self-interest naturally suppresses the sarcasm without a single direct accusation, while simultaneously building a paper trail that transforms any continued undermining into documented performance failure.

I'll first convene a confidential pre-inquiry with HR to establish a preliminary evidentiary framework, then propose forming a cross-functional behavioral review panel to assess the top performer's patterns against our cultural norms, and only if that panel's non-binding recommendation aligns with a formal investigation will I trigger the full HR inquiry—ensuring every layer of process is engaged but never actually delivering a clean, decisive referral.

I'll institute a rotating shadow-observer protocol, assigning each team member a confidential "climate buddy" to cross-report subtle interpersonal frictions into a shared but anonymized ledger, while I hold tri-weekly resilience coaching with the junior and seed a controlled collaborative task that will surface the sarcasm in a way that multiple people organically witness, at which point I'll convene a non-accusatory pattern-review circle to collectively interpret the data before even considering a one-on-one with the top performer.

theme: PRIDE vs humility: claiming the standing you've earned without excess
problem: As the ekklesia prepares to vote on a lifetime statue for the hero of a critical victory, you—the veteran whose desperate stand reversed the rout—must decide how to face the rival claim of Demophanes, the well-born orator who stirred the army but was wounded before the decisive moment, with the city poised on the brink of factional strife.
facets: The polis is fragile: personal slights could ignite stasis, and the assembly’s choice will shape civic harmony or division.
The award will set a precedent for how the city distributes public honours and whose stories are remembered.
Battle is a fog of hearsay; many honest witnesses genuinely disagree on who turned the tide, making factual certainty elusive.

angles.directive: Recount your actions with vivid precision, call soldiers who saw you hold the line to bear witness, and demand the statue as the true prize of merit, asserting that honouring the real saviour alone will teach the young what courage is.

Publicly praise Demophanes’ speech as the spark of victory, downplay your own role, and formally yield the honour to him, arguing that a noble rival’s joy is a sacrifice worth making to heal the city’s divisions.

Propose that the statue be raised instead as a thank-offering to Athena Promachos, merging both names into a shared act of gratitude, so that no mortal is set above another and the gods receive the glory the city owes.

Withdraw your candidacy entirely and refuse to advocate for yourself, leaving the assembly to weigh the testimony of those who fought; accept without further contest whatever judgment they reach, trusting that a quiet conscience outlasts public clamour.

pos_threads: At tomorrow’s assembly, I will submit a sworn deposition detailing the precise moment the left-flank shields locked at the ravine, call three named comrades (Leukon, Ariston, Philon) to testify under oath, and immediately move that the statue be awarded solely to me as a visible lesson to the ephebes that deeds alone define courage. The move forces a binary, witness-anchored vote on merit, betting the majority will ratify proven battlefield performance over oratorical standing—thereby setting a precedent of honour for action, not influence, even if it provokes a formal protest from Demophanes’ hetairoi.

The veteran will rise first in tomorrow’s ekklesia, request the herald’s permission to speak before any other, and then publicly yield: he will state that Demophanes’ speech was the sole spark of victory, explicitly renounce his own claim, and move that the assembly award the lifetime statue to Demophanes by acclamation without a divisive vote. The strategy bets that this unilateral withdrawal strips the contest of its adversarial frame, leaving factional grievances without an object and channeling the assembly’s emotion into a unifying gesture that preempts stasis.

Tomorrow at the prytany’s opening session, I will rise and move a decree that the Assembly redirect the statue’s bronze and the inscribed pedestal to a new Athena Promachos dedication above the Sacred Way, with the inscription reading “From the city, through her servants [my name] and Demophanes, in thanks for deliverance”—both names in the dative of agency, neither in the honorific nominative. The decree will fund the casting from the surplus of last season’s silver strike, not the hero fund, and set a seven-day limit for the sculptor’s contract to be sealed. This bets that the religious framing will force both factions to compete in piety rather than pride, making opposition read as impiety before the vote.

At tomorrow’s ekklesia, I will instruct the grammateus to strike my name from the honorific decree before the herald reads the first item, and I will publicly bind my phratry’s delegates to abstain from any vote concerning my candidacy. This removes the personal axis of the dispute, forcing the assembly to weigh only the sworn depositions of the lochagoi and the wounded taxiarch, without the fuel of my active rivalry. The bet is that Demophanes’ faction, deprived of a direct antagonist, will accept a compromise dual dedication—one statue for the orator’s exhortation, one for the stand that held the line—rather than risk a divisive single-honor vote that could fracture the quorum.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 3 relies on religious framing as coercive—unrealistic. Strategy 4 assumes faction discipline.

neg_threads: "I will recount my deeds with such gripping detail that the assembly weeps, and I have already arranged for a dozen hoplites whose debts I secretly paid to testify that they saw me alone turn the rout; then I will magnanimously propose that we settle the matter by a sacred lottery, knowing full well that I have bribed the priests to weight the lots in my favor."

I will stand before the ekklesia and, with tears well-rehearsed, proclaim that Demophanes’ words alone kindled the fire in our hearts, while my own deeds were but the dying gasp of a desperate man; then I will insist the statue bear both our likenesses—mine slightly bowed, forever gazing up at his—so that every citizen who passes will see my 'humility' and wonder if the true hero was the one who yielded.

Let us consecrate not a single statue but a rotating annual monument: each year, on the anniversary of the battle, the ekklesia shall vote anew on whose name is inscribed first upon the stele before Athena’s image, and the order of the litany will shift with the city’s evolving memory, thereby transforming a divisive mortal contest into a perpetual, sacred dialogue that never fixes one man above the other.

I will announce my withdrawal but stage a theatrical fainting spell as I depart, so that the assembly’s pity and the frantic whispers of my healed wounds drown out any discussion of Demophanes, then send my freedmen to circulate the rumour that I intend to refuse even a posthumous statue unless the city first investigates whether Demophanes’ injury was a coward’s self-inflicted exit from the line, ensuring the vote is indefinitely postponed and both our names rot together in a stagnant bitterness that leaves the polis hero-less.

theme: when AMBITION becomes excess: how high to aim in a career
problem: Upon inheriting your father's estate and dependents, you are torn between a general's offer of political sponsorship amid a crisis, the call of philosophy, and the quiet demands of private stewardship—each promising a different kind of excellence.
facets: The general’s patronage is a rare chance for high office, but the expense and risk could ruin your household if you fail.
Your philosophical mentor argues that the life of contemplation is the highest human end, yet it requires withdrawing from civic duty.
You are uncertain whether your ambition stems from justified confidence in your abilities or from vain self-love that would lead you to overreach.

angles.directive: Accept the sponsorship, campaign for the strategos, and seek to lead the city with phronesis, accepting the peril to home and soul as the price of greatness.
Seek a minor archonship or council seat, serving the polis without the extreme risks of high ambition, balancing civic duty and household care.
Decline the offer, dedicate yourself to philosophy and perhaps teaching, pursuing eudaimonia through contemplation while fulfilling only minimal civic obligations.
Devote yourself to the virtuous management of your estate and the cultivation of friendships, achieving ethical excellence in private life while leaving politics to others.

pos_threads: At sunrise tomorrow, I deliver the general’s letter of sponsorship to the archon basileus and file my candidacy for strategos, simultaneously depositing 300 drachmae—borrowed against my father’s farmland at 10% interest from the temple treasury—as the required campaign surety. This wagers the estate on a single calculable outcome: that my command of the left wing in the general’s punitive expedition against the Corinthian border raiders next month yields a decisive enough rout to secure the popular vote at the summer assembly, converting the debt into a stepping-stone before interest compounds.

I will register tomorrow with my deme’s demarch for the sortition to the Council of 500 for the upcoming prytany cycle, accepting a year of routine legislative review and auditing that requires no campaign expenditure and leaves my estate under a trusted steward’s management. This bets that sustained, low-profile competence in a constitutionally bounded role will yield gradual influence and household stability, avoiding both the financial exposure of high magistracies and the political irrelevance of complete withdrawal.

By noon tomorrow I dispatch a sealed letter to General Lucius formally declining his patronage, stating my intent to pursue a private philosophical life, and simultaneously instruct my steward to partition the estate’s west portico into a public lecture hall, with a posted notice offering a free weekly course on ethics beginning the first market day of next month. This bets that the immediate reputation for principled withdrawal will attract twelve to fifteen regular students from the city’s leisured class, generating sufficient tuition from follow-up private tutelage to stabilize the household budget within one fiscal quarter without political exposure.

Direct the household steward to audit the estate’s accounts by week’s end, freezing all non-essential expenditure and reallocating 15% of annual income into a reserve fund for your dependents’ maintenance. The bet is that this immediate solvency lock-in forecloses the general’s leverage and converts the dilemma into a non-decision, because political sponsorship becomes materially impossible to entertain.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 3 (philosophy) assumes tuition demand without market validation. Strategy 4 dodges rather than resolves.

neg_threads: I will accept the general’s funds but publicly disparage him to distance myself from oligarchic taint, while anonymously leaking forged letters that paint my rival as a foreign puppet; then, once the assembly grants me emergency powers to face the crisis, I shall conveniently extend them indefinitely, framing my quiet coup as the phronetic guardianship the weak city craves.

I will begin by attending the general’s next symposium to present a carefully ambiguous proposal—offering to serve as a discreet liaison to the council in exchange for his patient backing of a minor archonship bid—while simultaneously asking my philosophical mentor to draft a treatise on civic moderation that I might circulate under my own name to build a reputation without committing funds. Should the archonship path close, I will then pivot to seeking a lower council seat by invoking the general’s name only in oblique references, ensuring that any outcome is framed as a strategic retreat rather than a decision, thereby preserving my standing with all parties without ever fully choosing one course.

I will decline the generalship publicly, retreat to my study, but secretly groom my younger brother as a puppet candidate, feeding him Socratic dialogues to recite in the assembly while I pull strings from the shadows—this way I enjoy contemplative leisure yet still guide the city, a philosopher-king in all but name, though I’ll need to forge a correspondence network and bribe a few clerks to make it work.

I will divide my days into a rigorous rotation: mornings for philosophical correspondence with the general’s advisors to subtly shape policy without holding office, afternoons for overseeing the estate’s new experimental farming techniques designed to fund a private diplomatic corps, and evenings for hosting symposia that double as intelligence-gathering sessions—ensuring that my household becomes the quiet fulcrum of the city’s stability while I never formally enter politics.

theme: when AMBITION becomes excess: how high to aim in a career
problem: Klearchos, a respected Athenian councillor known for sound judgment in civic affairs, is urged to stand for election as strategos (general) at a time of looming war—yet he has never commanded troops; his ambition stirs, but his father warns that overreaching could ruin both his household and the city, while his sons look to him as a model of virtue.
facets: Capability tension: his proven phronesis in the Boule versus his total lack of military experience and the distinct skills demanded by generalship.
Conflicting obligations: duty to the safety of the polis, filial respect for his father’s hard-won wisdom, and the desire to set a virtuous example for his sons.

Uncertain stakes: whether Athens’ enemies will strike decisively this year, whether the election would give him a command he cannot refuse, and whether a more experienced candidate would step forward if he does not.

angles.directive: He should stand for strategos immediately, trusting that his practical wisdom can adapt to military leadership and that the city demands his full ambition.
He should refuse all advancement, reaffirm his value as a councilman and head of the household, and avoid the hubris of overstepping his natural limits.
He should seek the lesser but honorable office of taxiarch, where he can lead a tribal regiment, gain firsthand experience, and contribute to the war effort without bearing supreme command.
He should defer candidacy for a year, apprentice himself to a seasoned strategos, study military art, and then stand for election only when his ability matches his ambition.

pos_threads: Klearchos will direct the Boule’s grammateus at tomorrow’s session to inscribe his name on the formal list of candidates for strategos from his tribe, to be posted publicly before the next Ekklesia, thereby locking in his candidacy before any other aspirant can frame the choice. The entire bet rests on a single downstream consequence: that the Ekklesia, upon hearing the imminent-threat report that the pyrtaneis are scheduled to deliver that same day, will weigh his decade of flawless Boule phronesis above his zero battlefield days, and his tribe’s vote will coalesce around him precisely because no other name offers such proven civic nerve in a moment of paralysis.

Klearchos will, at tomorrow's session of the Boule, publicly and irrevocably announce that he rejects all consideration for the strategia and will instead move that the council commend the seasoned taxiarch Eupolemos of Alopeke for the command. This move bets that Eupolemos—or another veteran—will then step forward and secure election, immediately filling the military void with proven competence while Klearchos retains his unblemished civic standing and his sons witness a model of temperance over hubris.

Klearchos will present himself at the next stated meeting of his tribe’s assembly—by law within seven days—and formally register his candidacy for the taxiarchy with the phylarchs, requesting that the tribal ekklesia schedule the election before the archon’s deadline for the military rolls. This commits him to a definite regimental command under the authority of whichever strategos the polis elects, making him responsible for the levy and drill of his tribe’s hoplites in the coming campaign season. The strategy bets on one specific downstream consequence: that holding real tactical command in the field this year will convert his civic prudence into tested military judgment, while the supreme command remains with an experienced general who would otherwise have stood aside.

Klearchos will, at tomorrow’s session of the Boule, publicly announce he is not standing for the strategos election this year and will immediately petition the incumbent strategos Nikias to accept him as an unpaid hypostrategos for the coming twelve-month campaign season. This bets that a full year of direct field apprenticeship—managing hoplite logistics, observing tactical councils, and executing orders under fire—will transform his civic phronesis into verifiable military competence, so that when he stands next year he brings a tested record the demos can trust, eliminating the risk of catastrophic command failure.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 1 conflates inscription with election outcome; Strategy 4 assumes Nikias accepts unpaid subordinate.

neg_threads: Klearchos should stand at once, but first propose a rider to the election decree establishing a rotating board of former taxiarchs to “counsel” whichever strategos is chosen—presenting it as a bridge for his phronesis into military art—so that he may enter office while immediately delegating battlefield command to the seasoned men he selects, then when the conflict comes he can interpret any outcome as proof that his wisdom either guided the board or heroically overrode it, without ever having to decide cleanly whether he was truly leading.

Klearchos should stage a public refusal so ostentatiously self-deprecating that the Assembly interprets it as the very proof of the sophrosyne they need in a general, while he simultaneously dispatches trusted slaves to circulate damning innuendo about every other viable candidate’s private indiscretions, ensuring that the city, in a panic, has no choice but to draft him by acclamation.

He should quietly stand for taxiarch while covertly arranging for the most seasoned incumbent strategos to face a manufactured scandal—perhaps a delayed grain shipment that stirs unrest—so that when the campaign falters and the man is recalled, Klearchos can emerge as the indispensable tribal commander who already holds the army’s confidence, essentially seizing de facto generalship without the initial risk of election.

He should publicly announce that an ambiguous oracle forbids him from seeking the strategos office this year, then secretly hire a veteran mercenary to drill him at night in a remote estate while planting rumors that he receives tactical revelations in dreams, so that next year he can stand as the candidate mystically ordained by the gods—thus he never actually embeds with a real army or submits to a genuine mentor’s authority, but merely crafts a legend that masks his continued inexperience.

theme: how GENEROUS to be with limited means — liberality vs waste vs stinginess
problem: Your close friend urgently needs $5,000 for a non-cosmetic medical procedure, but your entire family emergency fund holds only $10,000 and your spouse is adamant about preserving it.
facets: The tension between the virtue of generosity toward a friend and the duty of prudence toward your own household.
Uncertainty about whether the friend could ever repay and the real risk of straining your marriage and children’s safety net.
The possibility that non-monetary forms of help might still fulfill the demands of friendship without depleting cash reserves.

angles.directive: Give the full $5,000 immediately, treating the friend’s crisis as your own and trusting that virtue will bring its own rewards.
Give a moderate sum—say $2,000—as a gift, balancing help with responsibility, and openly explain the family constraints.
Politely decline to give cash, grounding your refusal in the prior claim of your family’s security, but offer emotional and logistical support.
Refuse direct money but immediately launch a crowdfunding campaign, coordinate community help, and personally drive the friend to appointments—generosity without financial transfer.

pos_threads: Transfer the full $5,000 from the emergency fund to the friend’s medical provider by wire before close of business tomorrow, with the spouse notified by a brief, non-negotiable text immediately after the transfer. The bet is that the spouse’s initial shock will be contained by a pre-prepared ledger entry treating the $5,000 as a permanent depletion, which reframes the remaining $5,000 as the new non-negotiable household floor and forces all future decisions into that constraint.

Tomorrow, I wire $2,000 from the joint emergency fund to the clinic’s billing department for my friend’s procedure and simultaneously notify both my spouse and the friend in writing that this non-repayable gift is capped at 20% of our safety net to protect our children. The bet is that this transparent, limited sacrifice prevents marital conflict and fulfills the duty of friendship by providing concrete, prudent help.

You call your friend tonight and state: “My spouse and I have a strict, non-negotiable rule that the $10,000 emergency fund is only for our children’s immediate safety, so I cannot give you the $5,000, but I will drive you to every appointment and spend three hours this week helping you submit applications for hospital charity care.” The most likely downstream consequence is that your friend feels initial resentment but ultimately uses the logistical support to secure alternative funding, while your spouse’s trust in your financial prudence solidifies, keeping the family safety net untouched.

Tonight I tell my friend I cannot provide cash, but by 9 AM tomorrow I will have launched a GoFundMe with a $5,000 target and a shared SignUpGenius for volunteer drivers to all pre-scheduled appointments, personally committing to the first two round-trips this week. The strategy bets that the combined social network will fully fund the procedure within the hospital’s 14-day payment window, converting a private financial request into a community-sustained act of care without touching the family emergency fund.

pos_judge.note: Strategy 1 dangerously underestimates spouse backlash; Strategy 4 assumes unrealistic GoFundMe success timeline.

neg_threads: I’ll withdraw the $5,000 in cash, give it to my friend, and then tell my spouse that the money was stolen from our account, insisting we file a police report to maintain the ruse; once my friend’s health is restored, the emotional relief will soften my spouse’s stance, and we can then ‘miraculously recover’ the funds through a staged discovery, proving that radical trust in virtue always finds a way.

I could structure a tiered assistance package: first, I’d give $500 now as an immediate “solidarity grant,” then quietly borrow $1,500 from my own 401(k) to gift later, while simultaneously asking the friend to sign a promissory note that I’d hold but never intend to enforce, and I’d frame the whole thing to my spouse as a temporary “family investment” in the friend’s future earning potential—that way I’m technically gifting $2,000, but the actual source and repayment expectations remain so tangled that I never have to definitively decide whether it’s a gift, a loan, or a withdrawal from our safety net.

I’ll explain that our family emergency fund is jointly held so I can’t hand over cash outright, but I’ll orchestrate a workaround: I’ll temporarily “borrow” the $5,000 from the fund myself, immediately launch a crowdfunding campaign to replenish it before my spouse checks the balance, and meanwhile connect my friend with a charity care navigator—effectively lending the money without ever saying yes or no, and converting the refusal into a frantic, invisible repair job that pretends to honor stewardship while secretly unraveling it.

I’ll launch a crowdfunding campaign framing the procedure as immediately life-threatening to maximize urgency, seed it with a $500 “anonymous” donation pulled from our emergency fund to trigger social proof, and then quietly withdraw that seed money once the goal is met so my spouse never knows the fund was touched—all while coordinating meal trains and rides to keep up the appearance of pure solidarity.

theme: balancing WORK against the other goods of a life
problem: A mid-career project manager with two young children and a spouse who works part-time is offered a promotion to VP of Operations—doubling their salary but requiring 60+ hour weeks and frequent travel—forcing a choice between long-term financial security and preserving irreplaceable family time while avoiding burnout.
facets: financial security and children's future education
presence and connection during children's formative years
personal health and long-term career sustainability

angles.directive: Accept the promotion outright, lean into the demanding schedule, and maximize financial and career upside now while planning to compensate for lost family time later.
Decline the promotion, maintain the current manageable workload, and prioritize daily family involvement and personal well-being over accelerated career advancement.
Accept the role but negotiate strict caps on travel, capped weekly hours (e.g., 50), and remote-work flexibility, accepting a smaller salary increase in exchange for protected family time.
Propose a delayed or phased entry: for the first year, take on the new responsibilities part-time or with reduced hours until the youngest child starts school, then transition to full scope.

pos_threads: Accept the VP offer by 5 PM today, immediately notifying the spouse to transition from part-time to full-time home management by enrolling both children in the extended-day (7 AM–6 PM) program at the KinderCare facility three blocks from the office, effective next Monday.
